title: The question toolkit
pedagogy: Capstone review — no new vocabulary. Drills selection between อะไร / ไหน / ใคร / ทำไม / เมื่อไหร่ / ยังไง / เท่าไหร่ / กี่ / ไหม, recombining sentence frames from units 3–10 (อันนี้เท่าไหร่, ห้องน้ำอยู่ที่ไหน, มี…ไหม). All tones as previously derived; nothing new to verify.

exercises.title: Nine question words, one rule
body: The full set: อะไร what · ไหน where/which · ใคร who · ทำไม why · เมื่อไหร่ when · ยังไง how · เท่าไหร่ how much · กี่ how many · ไหม yes/no.
One rule runs them all: put the question word where the answer will go. No inversion, no helper verbs, no exceptions.
This lesson is pure sparring — old sentences, your full toolkit, no new words.

exercises.context: You're a woman at a fruit stall, pointing at a mango. You want the price.
exercises.question: Which question word does the job?

choices.thai: เท่าไหร่คะ
เมื่อไหร่คะ

exercises.explanation: Price → เท่าไหร่. ทำไมคะ demands the mango justify itself, and เมื่อไหร่คะ asks when — the vendor will check her watch.

exercises.meaning: How much is this one? (female speaker)
exercises.gloss: thing + this + how-much + female question particle
exercises.explanation: The shop-01 frame, female edition. ทำไม stayed on the bench — wrong tool for a price.

exercises.gloss: water-room + be-at + at + which + male polite particle
exercises.explanation: Places take ไหน, things take อะไร — the go-02 frame from a colder start: this time your ear had to catch every word.

exercises.context: You want to ask a shopkeeper IF she has water — a yes/no question, not an open one. You're a woman.
exercises.question: Which form asks yes-or-no?

choices.thai: มีน้ำไหมคะ
มีน้ำอะไรคะ

exercises.explanation: ไหม is the yes/no switch. มีน้ำอะไรคะ is real Thai too — but it asks WHICH drinks she has. And มีน้ำค่ะ answers a question nobody asked.
